assertions.title: Artifact completions that arrive after a turn still persist to the same signed-in session

steps.action: Create Firebase test user via REST Identity Toolkit
steps.observed: User val-artifact-r2-1773386066@test.local created, idToken received (1001 chars)

steps.action: Save turns via PUT /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/turns (simulating conversational turn completion)
Save artifact via PUT /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/artifacts AFTER turn save (simulating delayed artifact completion)
steps.expected: Artifact persisted to same session with 200 status and valid artifactId
steps.observed: 200 OK, artifact id=d09d2f05b00845b3931a029be8b1c668, filename=brainstorm-ideas.md, mimeType=text/markdown, label='Project Brainstorm Ideas'

steps.action: List artifacts via GET /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/artifacts to confirm artifact is attached to session
steps.expected: Artifact appears in session's artifact list with correct metadata
steps.observed: 200 OK, 1 artifact returned with correct artifactId, filename, mimeType, blobPath, and label

steps.action: Reopen session via GET /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id} to verify artifact persists after reopen
steps.expected: Session data returned with updatedAt reflecting artifact save time
steps.observed: 200 OK, session returned with updatedAt=2026-03-13T07:14:56.913753Z (matches artifact creation time)

steps.action: UI verification: Sign in with test user in chrome-devtools, open session from library
steps.expected: Session library shows the session, opening it shows artifact card with content
steps.observed: Session library displayed with 'Untitled session' entry. Opening session showed artifact card with 'Project Ideas' content, preview panel with rendered markdown, 2 conversation messages, and Download button

assertions.title: Reopened persisted sessions restore real artifact files, not just metadata

steps.action: List artifacts for reopened session via GET /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/artifacts
steps.expected: Artifact metadata returned with blobPath pointing to real Cloud Storage object
steps.observed: 200 OK, artifact returned with blobPath=brainstorm/sessions/.../artifacts/.../brainstorm-ideas.md, filename, mimeType, label all correct

steps.expected: Real file content returned matching original upload, not placeholder or empty
steps.observed:
200 OK, downloaded 70 bytes of markdown content: '# Project Ideas

1. AI assistant
2. Smart dashboard
3. Voice interface' — exact match with original

steps.action: Download artifact AGAIN after session reopen (second download to confirm persistence)
steps.expected: Same real content returned on subsequent download
steps.observed: 200 OK, content identical to first download — confirms Cloud Storage blob is real and persistent

steps.action: UI verification: Open session in chrome-devtools and verify artifact preview
steps.expected: Artifact card shows content preview, file name, size, and rendered markdown preview
steps.observed: Artifact card displayed with 'Project Ideas' text content, filename 'brainstorm-ideas.md', size '70 B', Download button. PREVIEW panel showed rendered markdown with h1 'Project Ideas' and list items: 'AI assistant', 'Smart dashboard', 'Voice interface'

steps.action: Verify conversation turns also restored alongside artifacts
steps.expected: Turns visible in UI after reopen
steps.observed: Conversation panel showed '2 MESSAGES' with 'YOU' and 'GEMINI' labels visible

evidence.network: GET /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/artifacts -> 200 (1 artifact with blobPath), GET /api/v1/live/brainstorm/sessions/{id}/artifacts/{aid}/download -> 200 (70 bytes, content match)

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-SESSION-009 confirmed that artifacts saved after a turn boundary persist to the same session and are visible after reopen (verified via API and UI). VAL-SESSION-010 confirmed that reopened sessions restore real artifact files from Cloud Storage with correct content — not just metadata. The Cloud Storage bucket gen-lang-client-0579048282.firebasestorage.app is now fully functional, resolving the round 1 blocker.
